label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 9

extraction.one_line_summary: MEINTJES Louis won from the breakaway on a brutally steep finish while EVENEPOEL Remco distanced GC rivals by 44 seconds on MAS Enric and 1:12 on ROGLIČ Primož.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled peloton despite losing SERRY Pieter to COVID, proving depth can absorb key domestique losses before mountains.
EVENEPOEL Remco attacked only when MEINTJES Louis already committed in break, using breakaway as pacing reference rather than chasing it down.
Crash in peloton before final climb opened gaps that prevented organized chase, rewarding teams with leaders positioned near front.
BATTISTELLA Samuele and JANSSENS Jimmy fractured breakaway arguing over work rate, suggesting coalition fragility on extended climbs favors solo strength.

rider_signals.MEINTJES Louis: positive: first Grand Tour stage win at age 30 on 24% gradients suggests rediscovered climbing form after career plateau
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: gained 44 seconds on MAS Enric and 1:12 on ROGLIČ Primož while looking comfortable, ominous before TT specialty
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: described as really struggling on steep finish, losing 1:12 to EVENEPOEL signals vulnerability on ultra-steep gradients
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: lost 44 seconds to EVENEPOEL despite similar climbing profile, suggests he cannot match accelerations on punchy finishes
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: neutral: mentioned as bitten by EVENEPOEL pace but context unclear if distanced significantly or merely suffering
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: finished 2:37 down in 12th despite winning this stage in 2018, major form drop on familiar terrain
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: grouped with suffering riders PADUN and VALVERDE on finish, indicating poor adaptation to explosive gradients
rider_signals.CONCA Filippo: positive: world junior champion positioning EVENEPOEL perfectly before attack shows Quick-Step tactical discipline despite SERRY absence
rider_signals.VINE Jay: neutral: wearing polka dot jersey but finished fifth behind GC podium, suggesting break-hunting form but not GC threat
